---
title: "Schema & Variable Dictionary"
author: "World Bank / UNHCR Joint Team"
date: today
---

::: {.callout-important}
## Status
This variable dictionary is a working draft. Confirm the final variable
list, value-label codes, and the precise definition of `welfare_self` with the
50by30 methodology team before treating it as authoritative.
:::

## Overview

Every harmonized file uploaded and approved through the pipeline must conform to
this schema. Variables are grouped into **mandatory** (must be present and
non-missing per the rules of the variable) and **optional** (included when
available in the source survey).

The **unit of observation** is the individual: one row per person, with
household-level variables (e.g. `welfare`, `hhid`) repeated across all
members of the same household.

## Conventions

- **Type** refers to the Stata storage type of the variable in the harmonized
  `.dta` file: `str#` (string), `byte`/`int`/`long` (integers), `float`/`double`
  (reals). Read the equivalents in R (`character`, `integer`, `double`) via
  [`haven`](https://haven.tidyverse.org).
- **Missing** — mandatory variables may not be missing except where noted. Use
  Stata's system missing (`.`) for genuinely unknown values; do not use numeric
  sentinels such as `-9` or `99`, or extended missing values (`.a`–`.z`).
- **Labels** — categorical variables must carry Stata value labels; the numeric
  codes below are authoritative and must match across all surveys.
- **Identifiers** are strings to preserve leading zeros and avoid precision loss.

## Mandatory Variables

| Variable | Label | Type | Unit / range | Missing | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hhid` | Household identifier | `str` | — | Not allowed | Unique per household within the survey; string to preserve leading zeros |
| `pid` | Person identifier | `str` | — | Not allowed | Unique per individual within the survey |
| `welfare` | Welfare aggregate | `double` | ≥ 0, LCU, annual per capita | Not allowed | Consumption or income aggregate from the survey's official poverty methodology; `welfare_type` records which |
| `welfare_type` | Welfare measure type | `byte` | `1`–`3` | Not allowed | `1` = Consumption, `2` = Income, `3` = Expenditure |
| `welfare_self` | Self-reliance-adjusted welfare | `double` | ≥ 0, LCU, annual per capita | Not allowed | Welfare measure used for the 50by30 self-reliance indicator |
| `weight` | Sampling weight | `double` | > 0 | Not allowed | Individual sampling/population weight |
| `weight_type` | Weight type | `byte` | `1`–`2` | Not allowed | `1` = Frequency weight (FW), `2` = Probability weight (PW) |
| `hsize` | Household size | `int` | ≥ 1 | Not allowed | Number of household members |
| `countrycode` | Country code | `str3` | ISO / WDI 3-letter | Not allowed | e.g. `AGO`, `ETH` |
| `year` | Survey year | `int` | 1990–2035 | Not allowed | Four-digit year of data collection |

## Optional Variables

| Variable | Label | Type | Unit / range | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `age` | Age | `double` | 0–120 years | Decimal allowed for children under 5 (per GMD 2.0); integer otherwise |
| `male` | Sex | `byte` | `0`–`1` | `0` = Female, `1` = Male |
| `urban` | Urban/rural | `byte` | `0`–`1` | `0` = Rural, `1` = Urban |
| `educat4` | Education (4 cat.) | `byte` | `1`–`4` | See value labels below |
| `empstat` | Employment status | `byte` | `1`–`4` | `1` = Employed, `2` = Unemployed, `3` = Out of labor force, `4` = Not applicable |
| `strata` | Sampling strata | `int` | — | Design variable for variance estimation |
| `psu` | Primary sampling unit | `long` | — | Design variable for variance estimation |

## Value Labels

Categorical variables must use these codes and labels exactly.

`welfare_type`

: `1` Consumption · `2` Income · `3` Expenditure

`weight_type`

: `1` Frequency weight (FW) · `2` Probability weight (PW)

`male`

: `0` Female · `1` Male

`urban`

: `0` Rural · `1` Urban

`educat4`

: `1` No education · `2` Primary · `3` Secondary · `4` Tertiary

`empstat`

: `1` Employed · `2` Unemployed · `3` Out of labor force · `4` Not applicable

## Validation

These variables and value-label codes are enforced in the harmonization scripts. Any
deviation from the schema will trigger an error and prevent the harmonized file from
being uploaded to Datalibweb. The harmonization scripts also check for missing
values in mandatory variables, and for negative or zero values in `welfare` and
`weight`. The scripts will also check that `welfare_self` is not greater than
`welfare`, and that `hsize` is consistent with the number of unique `pid`s per `hhid`.
